label: Palestinians Killed (Gaza)
source: Gaza Ministry of Health / UN OCHA / WAFA
contestNote: Israel disputes figures, argues Hamas fighters counted as civilians. Cumulative total since Oct 7, 2023; plus 14,222 missing/presumed dead under rubble. Lancet Mortality Survey (Mar 2026) estimates 75,200 violent deaths through Jan 5, 2025 — 34.7% higher than MoH for same period. 765 killed since Oct 2025 ceasefire (OCHA Apr 17 report).

label: Palestinians Wounded (Gaza)
contestNote: Figures may undercount due to overwhelmed health system; +37 injured Feb 26–Mar 5

label: Displaced Palestinians
contestNote: ~90% of Gaza's 2.1M population displaced at peak

label: Hostages Remaining in Gaza
source: Hostages and Missing Families Forum / IDF
contestNote: All 251 hostages accounted for by Jan 26, 2026: ~105 released Nov 2023 truce, 33 in Phase 1 (Jan 2025), 20 alive + 28 bodies in Oct 2025 deal, remainder died in captivity or rescued

label: Structures Destroyed/Damaged in Gaza
contestNote: By late 2024, est. 60% of all structures damaged or destroyed

label: Aid Trucks Entering Gaza (daily avg)
contestNote: Aid inflows declined 37% from Jan 11–Apr 11 vs. first three months of ceasefire: <105,000 metric tons vs. 167,600 metric tons required. ~102–122 trucks/day entering mid-April vs. 600+ needed. Zikim Crossing reopened Apr 12 on alternating days; Kerem Shalom (Karem Abu Salem) primary active crossing; Kissufim remains closed. Jordan B2B route: 60 trucks twice weekly. Fuel compliance at 14%. 2026 Flash Appeal $4.1B req, only 9.7% funded. ~129 community kitchens serve 1.25M meals/day.

label: Healthcare Facilities Damaged/Destroyed
contestNote: All 36 hospitals in Gaza damaged or destroyed; ~16 partially functional at peak

label: Population at IPC Phase 5 (Catastrophe) Risk
contestNote: Israel disputes famine characterization. 571,000 in emergency hunger (IPC Phase 4); ~1,900 at catastrophe-level (Phase 5). Aid at 37% of required levels (OCHA Apr 17). 77% of Gaza population facing acute food insecurity. Bread production 200 tonnes/day vs. 450 required. Tomato prices tripled. 80% unemployment. 101,000+ children 6–59 months face acute malnutrition. 2026 Flash Appeal $4.1B req, only 9.7% funded.
